what_it_does: This file is the acceptance ledger / truth-table for the 'Live, Guided, Right-Hand-Man Review Surface'. It lists every criterion that must be true for the surface to be considered done, grouping them by feature area (A–O). Each criterion is split into a FUNCTION face (real behavior verified by use) and a FORM face (operator-facing, design-system-shaped surface), and is tagged with reuse lift (READY/MODERATE/NET-NEW) and an honest status target (verified-by-use / code-complete-untested / needs-tim). It also records settled decisions, extensibility rules, and appended criteria from coverage rounds.
observations: Every criterion is required to have both a FUNCTION and a FORM line; function-only or form-only is not allowed.
Status marks are explicit: ✅ verified-by-use, 🟡 code-complete-untested, 🔴 needs-tim (never green-painted by the loop).
Reuse status is tagged per criterion: READY, MODERATE, or NET-NEW.
Settled decisions are baked in: simple consent (approve + revert), verified comprehension, scoped temporal deixis, voice-in included.
Extensibility is append-only: new coverage rounds add groups rather than filtering threads out as out-of-scope.
Groups A–I cover the original GRS surface; Groups J–N append vault-organ criteria; Round 2 adds K3/K4/M3/Group O.
F2 'GENERATE-FOR-MOCKUPS' is labeled the make-or-break criterion.
Many criteria reference specific file:line observations (e.g., suite.py:6287, bridge.py:780-793) as evidence.
The file is truncated mid-criterion K4.
There are very few ✅ statuses; most targets are 🟡 or 🔴.
I5 is a cross-cutting anti-parallel-system guard requiring subsystem reuse.
J1–J9 represent an organ-merge with the vault 'RHM Walkthrough & Review Organ' criteria.
K3 introduces a configurable `cast_posture` axis in MODE_REGISTRY and `rhm_config`.
standouts: The two-faced FUNCTION/FORM requirement and the 'needs-tim' status category.
F2 — GENERATE-FOR-MOCKUPS — is explicitly the make-or-break gate.
The vault-organ merge (J1–J9) adds queue, status lifecycle, human go-gate, branching, verdict+WHY, derived-from bind, guard()/POLICY wiring, and S1–S7 acceptance scenarios.
I5 — the anti-parallel-system guard — forbids duplicating subsystems.
K3 — the `cast_posture` config axis — makes cognition-cast enrichment tunable per mode/subtype.
conventions: Two-faced criterion structure: FUNCTION then FORM.
Status tags use emojis with defined meanings.
Evidence cited as Observed(file:line) or verified-by-use with provenance.
Append-only group numbering (A–O) for new coverage rounds.
No ranking or 'better/canonical' language; neutral, file-local descriptions.
Cross-references to other criteria rather than duplicating them.
concerns: The file is truncated at the end of Round 2, cutting off K4 mid-sentence.
Heavy reliance on external file:line references that are not shown here.
Many criteria depend on 'verified-by-use' runs that have not yet happened.
Dense jargon and acronyms (R2, RHM, STT, VAD, SSE/NDJSON, etc.) may impede readability.
The append-only rule risks scope creep if every found thread must become a criterion.
Status targets are mostly 🟡/🔴, suggesting the surface is far from done.
notes: This is the two-faced acceptance checklist for building a guided right-hand-man review surface, merging GRS and vault-organ criteria while forbidding green-painting and subsystem duplication.
questions: What is the exact remaining content of K4 after the truncation?
Where are the vault S1–S7 acceptance scenarios defined in detail?
What is the concrete design rubric / design-critic / design-lint process referenced repeatedly?
How is the `cast_posture` subtype resolution actually threaded into `cast_for_mode()`?
What is the final priority order among the many NET-NEW criteria?
How is 'verified-by-use' provenance recorded and audited?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the truth-table/bar for when the surface is done; the file matches that purpose, presenting grouped, two-faced criteria with status targets and verification rules.
apparent_themes: Two-faced verification: behavior plus operator-facing form.
Mockup actionability as the founding promise.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prevent the build from declaring itself done prematurely by forcing every feature to be proven by real use and shaped for a non-developer operator. It is also trying to unify two prior design artifacts (the GRS criteria and the vault organ criteria) into one growable ledger, and to keep the implementation reusing existing subsystems rather than inventing parallel ones.
novelty: The two-faced FUNCTION/FORM split combined with explicit 'needs-tim' status marks and an anti-green-painting rule; append-only coverage rounds as a unification mechanism; a configurable `cast_posture` axis for cognition-cast enrichment; and a make-or-break 'generate-for-mockups' criterion that turns mockup review into autonomous editable output.
contribution: A structured, status-honest completion checklist that ties each feature to both behavioral verification and operator-facing form, and that grows by appending new coverage groups rather than filtering scope, giving a fused system a single acceptance ledger for the guided review surface.
summary_for_embedding: A two-faced completion-criteria ledger for a guided right-hand-man review surface, grouping requirements A–O by feature area and tagging each with function/form, reuse lift, and honest verification status.
intention_for_embedding: To set the acceptance bar and prevent green-painting for the guided review surface build while unifying GRS and vault-organ criteria.
